hcd_data.architectural_style: Converted House-form, Detached (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. The property was purpose-built for commercial use on the ground floor, which has been modified through subsequent ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. With its visibly layered built form, the property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property retains many original building features, including its chamfered corner, commercial storefronts along Augusta Avenue and Oxford Street, second storey window openings, and third-storey mansard roof with dormers. Anchoring the southeast corner of Augusta Avenue and Oxford Street, the property supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Oxford Street.

photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade is occupied and readable from the street, with contemporary storefront alterations layered onto the historic building form.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple fascia and coping with limited historic trim visible.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible from the selected frontage view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative character is primarily expressed through the storefront treatment and surviving upper-storey massing.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Corner storefront shared between restaurant signage and neighboring dispensary signage.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Storefront and signage have been modernized from the original historic condition.
photo_observations.notes: [batch_010] Daytime view from Oxford St side confirms corner building with terrace/patio area on Oxford. Pink mural on upper side wall. White/black storefront at grade. La Cevicheria appears closed. Chamfered corner entry visible.

facade_description: 285 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its converted house-form, detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a white and black storefront at grade with a pink mural-covered upper side wall and dark roof shingles palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include bay window, original windows, cornice. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. The property was purpose-built for commercial use on the ground floor, which has been modified through subsequent ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. With its visibly layered built form, the property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property retains many original building features, including its chamfered corner, commercial storefronts along Augusta Avenue and Oxford Street, second storey window openings, and third-storey mansard roof with dormers. Anchoring the southeast corner of Augusta Avenue and Oxford Street, the property supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Oxford Street.
heritage_summary: The property was purpose-built for commercial use on the ground floor, which has been modified through subsequent ground floor alterations. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Oxford Street.
